mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any consecutive 3-year window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and recession risk, prompting equity deleveraging and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, directly reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition fails to produce negative returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding a 2 percent quarterly annualized pace signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, supporting short-term equity momentum through improved profit margins.
failure_condition: The signal reverses to negative returns during easing cycles, or 5-day forward returns show a negative correlation (Spearman rho < -0.05) with industrial production growth.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 225 reflects accelerating inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 50 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for lagged market returns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm genuine labor market absorption, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels directly expand aggregate corporate earnings capacity, creating a mechanical upward bias in short-term equity price discovery.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 levels (Spearman rho < 0.05) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index falling below 90.0 indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply chain disruption, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold condition fails to separate return distributions (KS test p > 0.05).
